submission_comments.comment: Hey Tee, your app is looking great so far. The mobile responsiveness and CSS are looking awesome! I did mark your assignment as incomplete because the CountryCard component is rendering all of the cards rather than a single card for each country. Please refactor the CountryCard component and let me or your instructors know if you have any questions! 😀

Great job fixing the code Tee! Your Version  0 is officially complete! 
Just a heads up, I noticed your Pages folder is outside of your src folder. It should normally be inside of your src folder, because that is where all your custom React code should go. I recommend you move it into the src folder, and you may have to update some import statements after moving it.

submission_comments.comment: form css and functionality not fully complete yet 👏 🙂

Hey Ainslie, great work so far! The CSS is looking great. I would suggest a couple of small changes.
1. For the country input, you have a dropdown menu. For this to be functional with your future versions, a dropdown would need to include every country name. An easier suggestion would be to change this to a text input to match the Figma designs.
2. I also noticed that you have the page set up with 2 columns. This is okay for now, but in future versions the saved countries will be above the form on the page.
Let me know if you have any questions/further clarification!  ☺️

submission_comments.comment:
Great job Stephanie!! This is looking really good so far! 
A little bit of constructive feedback: 


In your country_counts table, right now you have NOT NULL as the constraint for your country_name column. We don't want the possibility of having 2 rows of data with the same country_name, because that could mean we have 2 different counts for the same country. So, I recommend you use the constraint UNIQUE NOT NULL.

In your saved_countries table, right now you have NOT NULL as the constraint for your country_name column. We don't want the possibility of having 2 rows of data with the same country_name, because that could mean we have 2 different counts for the same country. So, I recommend you use the constraint UNIQUE NOT NULL.
In your products table, in your expiration_date column, you are missing the data type, constraints and sample values. 

Please make the changes listed above, and resubmit your data model when you're done. While it might seem like we're fussing over small details right now, it will make your life much easier next Monday when you convert this data model into SQL code. Good luck, you got this!

Nice job Stephanie!! You're almost there! You just need to fix one more thing: 


In your country_counts table, right now you have NOT NULL as the constraint for your country_name column. We don't want the possibility of having 2 rows of data with the same country_name, because that could mean we have 2 different counts for the same country. So, I recommend you use the constraint UNIQUE NOT NULL.


Please make the changes listed above, and resubmit your data model when you're done. While it might seem like we're fussing over small details right now, it will make your life much easier when you convert this data model into SQL code. Good luck, you got this!

Hey Stephanie, I think you made the change in the wrong place. 
 
You used the UNIQUE NOT NULL constraint on the country_name column in the users table. This is not correct, because it means no two users can be from the same country. 
 
On the other hand, you used the NOT NULL constraint on the country_name column in the country_counts table. This means that we could accidentally have multiple counts for the same country. So, in the country_counts table, the country_name column should be UNIQUE NOT NULL. 
 
Please let me know if you have any questions.

-- Insert one saved country where the country_name is Mexico  -- On conflict, if the country's name is already saved, do nothingINSERT INTO country_counts (country_name, count)VALUES ('Mexico', 1)ON CONFLICT (country_name)DO UPDATE SET count = country_counts.count + 1RETURNING count;
